text:
• Dwell Time = Average Wait Per Operation
How long each operation waits in the queue before it starts executing.
MongoDB Reports (in serverStatus)
"queues": {
"execution": {
"read" : {
"normalPriority": {
"totalTimeQueuedMicros": 5000000, // cumulative total (us)
"removedFromQueue": 1000
// ops that finished waiting
｝
}
}
Mongotune Calculates
Between tick T-1 and tick T:
delta_queue_time = totalTimeQueuedMicros|T] - totalTimeQueuedMicros[T-1]
= 500,000 us (total wait time added this second)
delta_tickets
= removedFromQueue[T] - removedFromQueue[T-1]
= 100 ops (operations processed this second)
per_ticket_micros = delta_queue_time / delta_tickets
= 500,000 / 100
= 5,000 us (5ms average wait per op)
Visual
Op 1:
Op 2:
Op 3:
Op 4:
(queued 4ms, then executed)
(queued 6ms, then
executed)
(queued 8ms, then
executed)
(queued 2ms, then
executed)
execution starts
L arrived in queue
Total queue time: 4 + 6 + 8 + 2 = 20ms
Operations:
4
Dwell time: 20ms / 4 = 5ms per op
Summary
Term
Meaning
totalTimeQueuedMicros
Cumulative sum of all wait times (ever)
removedFromQueue
Cumulative count of ops processed (ever)
per_ticket_micros
Average wait per operation (this tick)
rate
Queue time accumulating per unit wall time
Dwell time = "How long is each operation sitting in line before getting served?"
- Low dwell (< 1ms): Server keeping up fine
- High dwell (> 20ms): Operations piling up, server overwhelmed → trigger rate limiting
